title: Disk gate: change default HIVE_MIND_DISK_THRESHOLD 0.8→0.65 for safer headroom on isolation hosts
body:
## Request

Change the default disk queue-admission threshold from **0.8 → 0.65**.

`HIVE_MIND_DISK_THRESHOLD` currently defaults to `0.8` (set in #1981, raised from the older `0.9`). In practice `0.8` still leaves too little headroom on disk-constrained isolation hosts — tasks continue to be launched at 80% used and then die at ~100% while a `--isolation docker` task expands images/scratch. Operators keep having to manually lower it to `0.65` in their env to get a safe margin.

## Where

`src/queue-config.lib.mjs`:

```js
// line ~250 (thresholds.disk)
disk: getThresholdConfig('disk', 'HIVE_MIND_DISK_THRESHOLD', 'HIVE_MIND_DISK_STRATEGY', 0.8, 'enqueue'),
// line ~266 (legacy flat DISK_THRESHOLD)
DISK_THRESHOLD: getThresholdConfig('disk', 'HIVE_MIND_DISK_THRESHOLD', 'HIVE_MIND_DISK_STRATEGY', 0.8, 'enqueue').value,
```

Both `0.8` default literals should become `0.65`.

## Why 0.65

- `--isolation docker` tasks need substantial free disk for image layers + per-task scratch; starting at 80% used routinely fills the host before a task completes.
- `0.65` is the value operators already fall back to manually (e.g. the hive-control-center deployment), so making it the default removes a required override.
- The `enqueue` strategy is unchanged — tasks wait rather than being rejected, so a lower threshold just means the bot backs off earlier and resumes once disk frees up.

## Scope

- Update the two default literals above.
- Update any doc/comment referencing the `0.8` default (e.g. the `Issue #1981` comment block).
- No env-var/API change — operators can still override via `HIVE_MIND_DISK_THRESHOLD` or `HIVE_MIND_QUEUE_CONFIG`.

Follow-up to #1981.
